new_amendments.trigger: learning loop fires only at end-of-chunk; mid-chunk failures compound undetected
see_evidence: MindStudio: 'Add intermediate validation steps. Don't just validate final outputs.'
Galileo: 'A corrupted memory at step 5 doesn't just break that step — it poisons every subsequent reflection, plan, and action.'
Microsoft Azure: 'Mandatory gates make the orchestration synchronous at that step'
new_amendments.local_evidence: GW5F internal fail-closed attempt log shows failures not caught until end-of-chunk
new_amendments.rule: After each artifact in a chunk, evaluate IC-1 through IC-6 interrupt conditions. If any fires, pause, write INTRA_CHUNK_INTERRUPT_RECEIPT, execute W0-W1, then W2-W3 if defect/gap. Never silently continue.
new_amendments.regression_risk: Increases per-artifact overhead by ~1 pulse check. Mitigated: checks are context-only, no tool calls.

new_amendments.trigger: tool failure mode bundle was created manually; should have been auto-generated
see_evidence: AWS DevOps Agent: 'After DevOps Agent resolves three incidents, the Learning Agent identifies the recurring pattern and generates a learned skill'
Union.ai: 'Artifacts can serve as a versioned registry... making artifacts the contracts that define inter-workflow dependencies and automations'
new_amendments.local_evidence: GW5T tool_failure_mode_bundle_v1.zip created by Robert request, not by workflow automation. Same pattern occurred in GW5J (canmore routing) and GW5T (recursive archive extraction).
new_amendments.rule: When any failure class reaches threshold (2+ instances), autonomously generate named sidecar bundle with README, trigger evidence, governance artifacts, and SHA index. Write to /mnt/data/workflow_sidecars/. If blocked, write SIDECAR_BLOCKED_RECEIPT.
new_amendments.regression_risk: Sidecar generation adds ~1 tool call per threshold crossing. Bounded by threshold — not triggered on every failure.

new_amendments.trigger: every ChatGPT session cold-starts; amendment ledger and state re-read from bundle every time
see_evidence: Microsoft Azure: 'Capabilities like memory are increasing the value of signals loops. These technologies enable AI systems to retain context and learn from user feedback'
AddyOsmani: 'One of the most powerful mechanisms in agent loops is the use of persistent context files that carry knowledge forward between iterations'
new_amendments.local_evidence: CURRENT_BASELINE_POINTER_v12 and QUEUE_STATE_v10 exist precisely because cold-start requires re-establishing state. Claude memory system provides cross-session persistence that ChatGPT lacks.
new_amendments.rule: At end of every session, write CLAUDE_MEMORY_SYNC_v1.json with current_stage, baseline_sha, active_amendments, active_patterns, open_ic_triggers, pending_sidecars, known_blockers, next_chunk, session_note. Claude loads this into memory system as cross-session bridge.
new_amendments.regression_risk: Adds one file write per session. Zero regression risk — additive only.

new_amendments.trigger: governance runs at preflight and end-of-chunk only; no execution-time intervention
see_evidence: AI Runtime Infrastructure (arxiv 2603.00495): 'A distinct execution-time layer... actively observing, reasoning over, and intervening in agent behavior while the agent is running'
Oracle/LangChain: 'Agent middleware is emerging as the standard abstraction layer... intercepting the loop at before_model, after_model, and modify_model_request'
new_amendments.local_evidence: VIGIL paper identifies same gap: 'VIGIL operates primarily OUTSIDE the agent execution loop. Its diagnostic and recovery mechanisms are invoked after failures have been detected.' MetaBlooms has the same architecture gap.
new_amendments.rule: Run lightweight pre-artifact and post-artifact runtime pulse checks (RP-PRE-1 through RP-PRE-4, RP-POST-1 through RP-POST-4). Log to RUNTIME_PULSE_LOG_v1.jsonl. No external tool calls — context-only checks. Block on T1 failures. Flag on T2.
new_amendments.regression_risk: Adds 8 boolean checks per artifact. Context-only. No tool calls. Negligible overhead.
